title: Morris Worm Forensics
description: Investigate a safe simulation of a Morris Worm incident. The learner identifies network, process, and file-system artifacts related to a historical worm attack.

levels.title: Incident briefing
levels.content: A historical Morris Worm style incident was reported in the target network. Your task is to investigate the target host and collect evidence. Connect to the client host as the analyst and begin from there.

levels.title: Find the target
levels.content: Scan the target network and identify the host that represents the infected machine.
hints: The target network is behind the router.
Try scanning 10.1.20.0/24 from the client host.

levels.title: Suspicious port
levels.content: Find the TCP port used by the simulated worm transfer mechanism on the infected host.
hints: Use nmap or netstat/ss after connecting to the server.
The Morris lesson discusses a netcat listener.

levels.title: Artifact analysis
levels.content: Find the created attack artifact under /opt/morris. Submit the artifact filename.

levels.title: Final flag
levels.content: After confirming the process, port, and file evidence, recover the flag from the infected host.
hints: The flag is stored in the victim user's home directory on the server.
